title: Next Greater Numerically Balanced Number
description:
An integer x is numerically balanced if for every digit d in the number x, there are exactly d occurrences of that digit in x.

Given an integer n, return _the smallest numerically balanced number strictly greater than_ n_._

solutionCode.python:
```python
def build_array(nums):
    ans = []
    for i in range(len(nums)):
        ans.append(nums[nums[i]])
    return ans
```
    
    We initialize a new array `ans` with the same length as the input numbers array `nums`. Then, we iterate through each element `i` in the `nums` array. For each `i`, we use the index value stored in `nums[i]` to access the value at that index in `nums`. We then add this value to the `ans` array at the corresponding index `i`. Finally, we return the `ans` array containing the values determined by the permutation.
